research.title: Astro Content Layer: Type-Safe Content from Markdown to CMS to DB
research.whyTrending: The Content Layer API is Astro 5's headline feature — one typed, pluggable API over markdown, MDX, headless CMS, and databases. Core to why content teams pick Astro; strong SEO for 'astro content collections'.

seo.suggestedTitle: Astro Content Layer: Type-Safe Content From Anywhere

gaps: Guides show glob() for markdown but under-explain the loader ABSTRACTION (the point is any source, one typed API)
file() loader for structured data often skipped
Writing a CUSTOM loader (CMS/DB) rarely shown concretely
The Astro 6 changes (legacy collections removed, z deprecated for astro/zod, live collections stable) not reflected in older posts

officialSources.note: Content Loader API: glob() and file() built-in loaders, custom loader contract

expertInsights: Content Layer = one typed, pluggable API over any content source (markdown, MDX, CMS, DB, API)
glob() loader: one entry per file from a glob pattern - blog posts, docs, projects
file() loader: multiple entries from a single structured file - data tables
defineCollection({loader, schema}) with a zod schema = validated frontmatter + full TS autocompletion
Custom loaders load from CMS (Contentful/Sanity/Storyblok), databases (PocketBase), or any API
Query with getCollection(name) and getEntry(name, id); reference() links collections (relations)
Content Layer: ~5x faster builds, ~50% less memory vs legacy collections
Astro 6 (Mar 2026): legacy collections removed by default; z from astro:content deprecated in favor of astro/zod; live collections stable
commonProblems: Treating Content Layer as just markdown loading (missing the any-source point)
Not validating with schema -> runtime frontmatter bugs
Using deprecated z from astro:content on Astro 6
Reaching for a custom loader when glob/file already fit
statistics: ~5x faster builds, ~50% less memory vs legacy

strategy.angle: Frame the Content Layer as an ABSTRACTION, not a markdown feature: one typed API (loader + zod schema) over any source. Show glob() and file() for local, then a custom loader for a CMS/DB to prove the point, cover querying + relations, and flag the Astro 6 changes (astro/zod, legacy removed, live collections) that make older tutorials wrong
strategy.title: Astro Content Layer: Type-Safe Content From Markdown, CMS, or a Database
strategy.excerpt: The Content Layer is Astros best idea: one typed, schema-validated API over any content source. Markdown, a headless CMS, a database - same loader contract, same getCollection call, full TypeScript autocompletion. Heres how it works, including the Astro 6 changes older guides miss.
hooks: Contrarian: the Content Layer isnt a markdown feature, its an abstraction - the source becomes an implementation detail
Practical: glob() for files, file() for data, a custom loader for your CMS - all behind one typed API
Gotcha: Astro 6 removed legacy collections and moved z to astro/zod - half the tutorials you find are already wrong
outline: Hook: your content lives in five places, your code shouldnt care
What the Content Layer actually is
file(): structured data in one file
The schema is the point: zod-validated frontmatter
A custom loader: pulling from a CMS or database
